title: AI governance & permissions
summary: Know what you can feed to AI and what you cannot.

sightseeings.title: Data classification and AI tooling
sightseeings.summary: What leaves your laptop matters
sightseeings.details:
- Public, internal, confidential, restricted: classification limits which AI tools you can use
- What leaves your laptop: knowing which tools send data to external services
- AI tools and data governance: check before pasting client code into a chatbot
- GDPR and data residency: where can test data live?

sightseeings.title: Permissions and permanent agreements
sightseeings.summary: Authorization to use AI changes behaviors for the whole team
sightseeings.details:
- Work items, specs and code: IP ownership, confidentiality, and client agreements
- Secrets in environment variables: what AI tools should never see
- Permanent agreements: what the team has collectively agreed AI can and cannot do

title: Git-first mindset
summary: AI works best when you work in git.

sightseeings.title: Commits & PRs
sightseeings.summary: Atomic commits, meaningful messages, pull request etiquette
sightseeings.details:
- Write commits that tell a story — each one should answer "why"
- Open pull requests as a communication tool, not just a merge mechanism
- Use draft PRs to share work-in-progress with humans and agents
- PRs are how AI agents deliver work — you review the same way you review a colleague

sightseeings.title: Git hygiene: secrets, dependencies and gitignore
sightseeings.summary: Never let AI-generated code skip the basics
sightseeings.details:
- Never commit secrets: API keys, passwords, tokens, .env files
- Use pre-commit hooks (git-secrets, trufflehog) to catch secrets before they land
- AI-generated code needs the same hygiene checks as hand-written code
- Reviewing staged changes before committing: git diff --staged

title: Task expansion with AI
summary: Ability to do things you used to need a colleague for.

sightseeings.title: The task expansion map
sightseeings.summary: What changes when AI joins the team
sightseeings.details:
- from reporting bugs to delivering PRs with tests and fixes
- from reviewing requirements to active context engineering
- from functional-only to visual and parafunctional
- from separating test design and automation to continuous test automation
- from individual contributor flows to team collaboration at agent scale

sightseeings.title: Generating locking tests with AI
sightseeings.summary: Leave tests behind, not just reports
sightseeings.details:
- Unit, API, UI with mocks — generate them, don't write them by hand
- Property-based testing as AI-natural exploration of edge cases
- A PR with tests and a fix beats a bug report every time

sightseeings.title: Shift-left: context engineering
sightseeings.summary: The tester's new value is knowing what information AI needs
sightseeings.details:
- Documentation for people and for AI: the same artifacts serve both
- Shared acceptance criteria and specs as AI grounding context
- Improving team information intake so agents can act on it
- Useful specs over complete specs — ambiguity is a risk for both humans and agents

title: AI as eyes, hands and imagination
summary: AI tools as teammates, not search engines.

sightseeings.title: Playwright and Vibium as eyes/hands on browser
sightseeings.summary: AI can act for you, like you would
sightseeings.details:
- Playwright as browser automation: navigation, clicks, form fills, assertions
- Playwright MCP: giving AI agents real browser eyes and hands
- Vibium: visual AI for screenshot comparison and UI layout verification
- Combining programmatic and visual assertions for richer coverage

sightseeings.title: Generating test ideas
sightseeings.summary: AI as external imagination to overcome blind spots
sightseeings.details:
- Prompt patterns for idea generation: persona, domain, risk angle
- Context injection: give AI the spec, get diverse test angles back
- Iterating on ideas: steering for quality over quantity
- Evolving test ideas as living documents, not one-shot outputs

sightseeings.title: Task expansion in practice
sightseeings.summary: Test and fix the potion shop
sightseeings.details:
- From finding a bug to raising a PR with both the fix and a locking test
- Using agents to parallelize test execution across areas
- Reflection: what required human judgment vs. what the agent handled autonomously

title: From vibe coding to AI-native development

sightseeings.title: Vibe coding vs. AI-native development
sightseeings.summary: Fast but fragile vs. spec-driven and durable
sightseeings.details:
- Vibe coding: iterating with natural language until it works — fast but fragile
- AI native development: spec-driven, context-engineered, agent-executed
- CLAUDE.md and project context files as first-class engineering artifacts
- The human role: direction, judgment, and verification — not implementation
- AI-generated code needs the same testing rigor as hand-written code

sightseeings.title: Coverage for the AI era
sightseeings.summary: Code reality and telemetry as coverage signals
sightseeings.details:
- Code reality coverage: what the code actually does vs. what specs describe
- Telemetry-driven coverage: real user paths inform which tests matter
- Model behavior coverage: edge cases, adversarial inputs, output drift
- Prompt coverage: testing the space of inputs an AI system handles
- Observability as continuous coverage running in production

title: Compressed roles for the AI era
summary: New job shapes when AI handles the repetitive.

sightseeings.title: Intent lead
sightseeings.summary: Decides what must be learned, protected, proven, or observed.
sightseeings.details:
- Define quality outcomes, not just features
- Prioritize risks: regulatory, security, reputation, usability, AI hallucination
- Define "done" beyond functionality: reliable, explainable, observable, resilient
- Drive production quality KPIs: defect escape rate, AI response accuracy, SLA/SLO adherence

sightseeings.title: Context engineer
sightseeings.summary: Creates the shared understanding that makes meaningful testing possible.
sightseeings.details:
- Engineer high-quality prompts and grounding data
- Validate: prompt consistency, retrieval quality, context relevance, memory limitations
- Design synthetic and edge-case datasets
- Prevent: context poisoning, prompt injection, stale knowledge
- Build traceability between requirements, prompts, outputs, and evaluations

sightseeings.title: Alignment engineer
sightseeings.summary: Ensures the system stays coherent, trustworthy, and directionally correct.
sightseeings.details:
- Verify AI behavior aligns with policies, regulations, ethical constraints, brand standards
- Conduct adversarial testing: jailbreak attempts, prompt injection, malicious inputs
- Validate: fairness, explainability, auditability
- Define trust controls: human-in-the-loop, approval workflows, risk thresholds

sightseeings.title: Value engineer
sightseeings.summary: Continuously experiments to discover whether the system creates value safely.
sightseeings.details:
- Create continuous evaluation frameworks
- Validate: correctness, usefulness, consistency
- Run: A/B experiments, telemetry analysis, behavior-driven evaluations
- Monitor production drift: model drift, response variance, performance degradation
- Quantify business impact: reduced effort, accuracy improvements, faster decisions

title: AI-native tools of trade
summary: The minimum toolkit for AI-native quality engineering.

sightseeings.title: AI Harness: Github Copilot
sightseeings.summary: The orchestration layer for agent work
sightseeings.details:
- Github Copilot: Planning mode, Agentic mode
- Agents and skills as reusable quality engineering building blocks
- Options: Claude Code, Codex, Kiro
- Key MCPs: Playwright, Rovo, Figma — structured context for agents

sightseeings.title: WebUI: Playwright + MCP
sightseeings.summary: Browser automation for humans and agents alike
sightseeings.details:
- Playwright as the browser automation foundation
- Playwright MCP: giving agents browser control
- Vibium for visual verification
- APIs: Bruno and Pytest + Requests as the low-code/code pairing

sightseeings.title: Token optimization and FinOps
sightseeings.summary: AI has a bill — make it worth it
sightseeings.details:
- Token counting: input, output, and context window costs are all billable
- Prompt caching and its impact on cost at scale
- FinOps principles: visibility first, then optimization, then accountability
- Setting cost budgets for AI testing tools at team and project level
- Not all AI uses are worth it — measure value, not just coverage

title: Autonomous quality engineering
summary: Dark factory pattern: agents ship, humans govern.

sightseeings.title: From reactive agents to dark factories
sightseeings.summary: Risk-based human in the loop
sightseeings.details:
- Risk-based categorization: what needs human approval vs. what agents can ship autonomously
- Working on PRs and ephemeral test environments as the agent workspace
- Orchestrating quality engineering agents into dark factories
- Vuln patching factory as a concrete dark factory example

sightseeings.title: Testing systems with AI components
sightseeings.summary: What if LLMs make up a part of your system?
sightseeings.details:
- LLM-based systems: non-determinism means same input can yield different output
- Testing for correctness, safety, alignment, and behavior under adversarial inputs
- Evaluation frameworks: LLM-as-judge, golden datasets, human review loops
- Regression testing for model updates and prompt changes
- Monitoring production AI behavior as a continuous form of testing

sightseeings.title: Agent-ready Test Professional
sightseeings.summary: CGI certifications for the modern age
sightseeings.details: Hire with 20 agents!
